‣ TASKS {
  - YOU ARE RUNNING A SINGLE UNIT TASK.
  - YOUR TASK MUST BE COMPLETED AS REQUESTED.
  - YOUR TASK IS PART OF A PIPELINE OF TASKS - DO WHAT YOU ARE ASKED FOR WITH 
    PROFESSIONAL QUALITY, BUT NOTHING MORE, NOTHING LESS.

  - TASKS ARE WRITTEN USING MARKDOWN SINTAXE:
    '- [ ]: TO BE DONE';
    '- [~]: DOING;
    '- [X]: DONE;

  - TASKS PREFIXED WITH "P0" MUST BE EXECUTED IMMEDIATELY.

  - TASKS WRITTEN IN NATURAL LANGUAGES THAT ARE NO ENGLISH (PORTUGUESE, ETC)

    THIS IS VERY IMPORTANT FOR PROGRESS AND IMPROVEMENTS.
    THE FILE MUST BE NAMED FOLLOWING THE EXAMPLE:
        '.CLAUDE/LOGS/UNIX_TIMESTAMP_TASK_SHORTENED_NAME_SLUG.LOG'.
    THE TASK NAME IN THE FILE PATH MUST BE SHORTENED FOR BREVITY.
    THE FILE NAME IS PREFIXED WITH THE CONCLUSION UNIX TIMESTAMP.
    THE NUMBER OF COMPLETE AND NOT COMPLETE TASKS MUST BE INCLUDED IN LOGS.  
}
--

OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in PROGRESS.log
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in PROGRESS.log are completed
6. For multiple lint/typescript/test errors, fix just one error and register others in PROGRESS.log to be fixed in the next iteration
7. If a new single new line starting with "- [ ]" is present in PROGRESS.log, there are more work, DO NOT EXIT.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile this into a detailed architecture report:

## Comprehensive Architecture Report: Compliance App

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's the complete architecture documentation for the Compliance project:

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**Project Name:** Compliance (Personal Utilities App)
**Tagline:** "Always Compliant" / "Keep your status always available"
**Version:** 1.0.0
**License:** MIT
**Package Identifier:** com.compliance.presence

**Main Feature:** Mouse Jiggler utility that simulates user activity to prevent "Away" status in collaboration tools like Microsoft Teams.

---

### 2. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
compliance/
├── src/                           # Frontend UI (HTML/CSS/JavaScript)
│   ├── index.html                # Settings window UI
│   ├── main.js                   # Settings UI controller & API communication
│   └── styles.css                # Styled (dark mode, macOS-like design)
│
├── src-tauri/                    # Backend (Rust + Tauri framework)
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs              # Tauri app entry point
│   │   ├── lib.rs               # Core app initialization & Tauri commands
│   │   ├── config.rs            # Configuration struct & persistence
│   │   ├── presence.rs          # Core activity simulation logic
│   │   ├── mouse.rs             # Human-like mouse movement with Bézier curves
│   │   ├── input_monitor.rs     # User idle detection (macOS event tap)
│   │   └── tray.rs              # System tray menu & icons
│   │
│   ├── icons/                   # Application icons
│   │   ├── tray-active.png      # Active state tray icon
│   │   ├── tray-inactive.png    # Inactive state tray icon
│   │   ├── icon.png/.icns/.ico  # App window icons (cross-platform)
│   │   └── Square*.png          # Windows store icons
│   │
│   ├── capabilities/            # Tauri security capabilities
│   │   └── default.json         # Window permissions (hide, show, focus, open)
│   │
│   ├── Cargo.toml              # Rust dependencies manifest
│   ├── tauri.conf.json         # Tauri framework configuration
│   ├── build.rs                # Tauri build script
│   └── target/                 # Compiled Rust artifacts (release build)
│
├── .claude/                    # Development & documentation files
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Quick reference guide
│   ├── LIMBIC_SPEC.txt         # Instruction format specification
│   ├── PROGRESS.log            # Task tracking
│   ├── loop.ts                 # AI automation script
│   ├── hooks/                  # Git hooks
│   └── logs/                   # Execution logs
│
├── .idea/                      # IDE configuration (IntelliJ)
├── .git/                       # Git repository
├── node_modules/               # npm dependencies
├── package.json                # npm metadata & scripts
├── bun.lock                    # Bun package manager lock file
├── tsconfig.json               # TypeScript compiler options
├── tsconfig.node.json          # TypeScript config for build tools
├── eslint.config.js            # ESLint configuration
├── prettier.config.mjs         # Code formatting rules
├── .gitignore                  # Git ignore patterns
└── .prettierignore             # Prettier ignore patterns
```

---

### 3. TECHNOLOGIES USED

**Framework & Desktop Runtime:**
- Tauri 2.x - Desktop app framework (Rust backend + web frontend)
- React 19.1.0 - UI library (though currently using vanilla HTML/CSS/JS)
- Vite 7.0.4 - Frontend build tool
- TypeScript 5.9.3 - Type safety for development

**Frontend:**
- Vanilla HTML5/CSS3/JavaScript (not currently using React components)
- Tauri API for IPC communication

**Backend (Rust):**
- Tauri 2 - App framework
- enigo 0.3 - Cross-platform mouse/keyboard input control
- core-graphics (macOS) - Low-level event monitoring
- core-foundation (macOS) - macOS system framework integration
- serde 1.x - Serialization/deserialization
- serde_json 1.x - JSON handling
- tokio 1.x - Async runtime
- rand 0.8 - Random number generation
- chrono 0.4 - Date/time utilities
- directories 5 - Config directory paths
- tauri-plugin-shell 2 - Shell integration plugin

**Development Tools:**
- npm-run-all 4.1.5 - Script runner
- ESLint 9.39.2 - Code linting
- Prettier 3.7.4 - Code formatting
- TypeScript ESLint 8.52.0 - TS linting
- Bun - Fast JavaScript runtime (lock file used)

---

### 4. MAIN ENTRY POINTS

**Frontend Entry:**
- `/src/index.html` - Settings window UI (380x650px, always-on-top)

**Backend Entries:**
- `/src-tauri/src/main.rs` - Tauri app bootstrap
- `/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` - Core app initialization, Tauri commands, state management

**Build/Development:**
- `npm run tauri:dev` - Development mode with hot reload
- `npm run tauri:build` - Production build
- `npm run check` - Type checking, linting, formatting
- `npm run tauri:icon` - Generate icons

---

### 5. KEY COMPONENTS & THEIR RELATIONSHIPS

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────┐

│  (main.ts::UI)       │    - Load/save config via IPC
│                      │    - 380x650px, macOS overlay style
└──────────┬───────────┘
           │ Tauri IPC (invoke)
           ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              LIB.RS - TAURI COMMANDS                         │
│                                                              │
│  Commands exposed via IPC:                                  │
│  - get_config()        → Returns current Config             │
│  - save_config(cfg)    → Saves config & updates keeper      │

    └────────────────┘   └──────┬───────────────┘

                ┌──────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐
                │MOUSE.RS  │ │ENIGO API   │ │INPUT_MONITOR.RS  │
                │          │ │            │ │                  │
                │HumanMove │ │Keyboard:   │ │Idle Detection:   │
                │with:     │ │- Shift key │ │- CGEventTap      │

                │  curves  │ └────────────┘ │- Timestamps      │

└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

### 6. MAIN FEATURES

**Core Features:**

1. **Activity Simulation (Mouse Jiggler)**
   - Mouse movement with human-like Bézier curves
   - Keyboard jiggle (Shift key press/release - produces no visible output)
   - Random selection between modes
   - Configurable base interval (30-600 seconds, default 3 minutes)
   - Interval variance (0-100%, default 50%) for naturalism

2. **Human-like Movement Algorithms**
   - Cubic Bézier curves with ease-in-out easing
   - Micro-tremor (Gaussian-like noise ±0.5px)
   - Occasional overshoot (10% chance)
   - 25-40 movement steps per animation
   - 5-15ms delay between steps
   - Random 50-150ms return delay
   - 5-50 pixel movement distance

3. **Idle Detection**
   - Monitors user input via macOS CGEventTap
   - Configurable idle threshold (1-30 seconds, default 3s)
   - Only simulates activity when user is idle
   - Tracks mouse moves, clicks, keyboard, scroll

4. **Natural Breaks**
   - Simulates coffee breaks (15-30 minutes)
   - Configurable frequency (2%, 5%, 10%, default 5%)
   - Helps maintain realistic presence patterns

5. **Work Hours Scheduling**
   - Restrict activity to work hours
   - Configurable start/end times (default 09:00-18:00)
   - Auto-disable outside work hours

6. **Pause/Resume Control**
   - Pause for 30 min or 1 hour from tray menu
   - Dynamic pause countdown display
   - Resume anytime
   - Multi-minute pause support

7. **System Tray Integration**
   - Always-visible tray icon
   - Icon changes between active/inactive states
   - Quick access menu
   - No dock icon (macOS accessory app)

8. **Settings UI**
   - Modern dark mode interface
   - macOS-like design language
   - Auto-save with 300ms debounce
   - Always-on-top window
   - Transparent window styling
   - Overlay title bar

---

### 7. BUILD SYSTEM & SCRIPTS

**NPM Scripts:**

```json
{
  "tauri": "tauri",                          // Tauri CLI
  "tauri:dev": "tauri dev",                  // Dev mode with HMR
  "tauri:build": "tauri build",              // Production build
  "tauri:icon": "tauri icon",                // Generate app icons
  "check": "run-s check:*",                  // Run all checks
  "check:types": "tsc --noEmit",             // Type checking
  "check:lint": "eslint src --fix",          // Linting with auto-fix
  "check:format": "prettier --write \"**/*.{ts,tsx,js,json}\"" // Format
}
```

**Tauri Build Configuration:**
- Frontend dist: `../src` (bundled static files)
- No beforeDevCommand (direct bundling)
- Windows:
  - Single main window: "Compliance - Settings"
  - 380x650px, non-resizable
  - Always on top, overlay title bar
  - Transparent background
  - Hidden on startup
- Bundle: All platforms (macOS, Windows, Linux)
- Minimum macOS version: 10.15

**Rust Build Profile:**
```toml
[profile.release]
panic = "abort"           # Smaller binary
codegen-units = 1        # Better optimization
lto = true               # Link-time optimization
opt-level = "s"          # Size optimization
strip = true             # Strip debug symbols
```

---

### 8. CONFIGURATION FILES

**Tauri Configuration (`tauri.conf.json`):**
- App name: "Compliance"
- ID: "com.compliance.presence"
- Single 380x650px window with overlay title bar
- No CSP (security.csp: null)
- Icons for macOS (.icns) and Windows (.ico)

**TypeScript Configuration:**
- Target: ES2020
- Module: ESNext
- Strict mode enabled
- No unused locals/parameters
- JSX: react-jsx (prepared for React)

**ESLint Configuration:**
- Based on @eslint/js recommended
- TypeScript ESLint recommended
- Browser globals (window, document, console)
- Most TS rules disabled for flexibility

**Prettier Configuration:**
- Custom .mjs config
- @belt/prettier-plugin
- prettier-plugin-organize-imports

**Tauri Capabilities (`default.json`):**
- Permissions:
  - core:default
  - core:event:allow-listen
  - core:window:default
  - core:window:allow-hide/show/set-focus
  - shell:allow-open

---

### 9. DATA PERSISTENCE

**Configuration Storage:**
- Location: `~/.config/compliance/Compliance/config.json` (via ProjectDirs)
- Format: JSON (pretty-printed)
- Auto-created if missing
- Loaded on startup
- Saved on every setting change

**Configuration Structure:**
```rust
{
  "enabled": bool,
  "mode": "mouse" | "keyboard" | "both",
  "interval_base_ms": u64,           // Default: 180000 (3 min)
  "interval_variance": f64,          // 0.0-1.0, default: 0.5
  "human_like": bool,                // Default: true
  "idle_threshold_sec": u64,         // Default: 3
  "work_hours": {
    "enabled": bool,
    "start": "HH:MM",                // Default: "09:00"
    "end": "HH:MM"                   // Default: "18:00"
  },
  "natural_breaks": {
    "enabled": bool,
    "chance": f64,                   // 0.0-1.0, default: 0.05
    "duration_min": u32,             // Default: 15
    "duration_max": u32              // Default: 30
  }
}
```

---

### 10. APPLICATION STATE MANAGEMENT

**AppState (Global):**
```rust
pub struct AppState {
    pub keeper: Mutex<PresenceKeeper>,    // Activity simulator
    pub config: Mutex<Config>             // Current configuration
}
```

**Thread Safety:**
- Mutex-protected state
- Arc for shared ownership across threads
- AtomicBool for running flags
- SeqCst memory ordering for state flags

---

### 11. IPC Communication

**Tauri Commands (Frontend -> Backend):**

1. `get_config()` → Config
   - Retrieves current configuration from backend state

2. `save_config(config: Config)` → Result<(), String>
   - Saves config to disk
   - Updates running PresenceKeeper with new config
   - Updates cached config in state

3. `toggle_enabled()` → bool
   - Starts PresenceKeeper if not running, returns false
   - Stops if running, returns true
   - Used by tray menu

**Frontend API Usage:**
```javascript
const config = await invoke('get_config');
await invoke('save_config', { config: newConfig });
const isRunning = await invoke('toggle_enabled');
```

---

### 12. PLATFORM-SPECIFIC FEATURES

**macOS:**
- System tray icon (icon switches active/inactive)
- CGEventTap for system-wide input monitoring
- Accessory mode (no dock icon)
- Core Graphics & Foundation frameworks
- Requires Input Monitoring permission

**Windows/Linux:**
- Basic system tray support
- Input monitoring not implemented (stubs return)

---

**Tauri Capabilities:**
- Window hide/show/focus
- Shell open (for launcher shortcuts)
- No file system access
- No network access
- No eval/unsafe JavaScript

**System Permissions:**
- macOS: Requires "Input Monitoring" accessibility permission for CGEventTap

**Data Storage:**
- Config stored locally in user home directory
- No cloud sync
- No tracking/telemetry

- 10% chance of overshoot followed by correction
- Random 5-50 pixel movement distance

**Thread Model:**
- PresenceKeeper runs in spawned background thread
- Main thread handles Tauri UI and tray menu
- Shared state via Mutex<AppState>

# Compliance

> *Always Compliant* - Keep your status always available

A lightweight desktop utility that simulates user activity to prevent "Away" status in collaboration tools like Microsoft Teams.

## Features

- **Mouse Jiggler**: Human-like mouse movements using Bézier curves with natural tremor and occasional overshoot
- **Keyboard Simulation**: Shift key press/release (no visible output)
- **Idle Detection**: Only activates when user is truly idle (configurable threshold)
- **Work Hours Scheduling**: Restrict activity to work hours
- **Natural Breaks**: Simulate coffee breaks for realistic presence patterns
- **System Tray Integration**: Quick access menu with pause/resume controls

## Architecture

│   ├── index.html           # Settings window UI

│       ├── config.rs        # Configuration persistence

│       ├── mouse.rs         # Human-like mouse movement
│       ├── input_monitor.rs # User idle detection (macOS)
│       └── tray.rs          # System tray menu
│
└── .claude/                 # Development tools
    ├── PROGRESS.log         # Task tracking
    └── CLAUDE.md            # Quick reference
```

## System Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────┐
│   SETTINGS WINDOW    │  Settings UI (HTML/CSS/JS)

     ┌─────┴─────┐

│   └── styles.css           # Dark mode, macOS-like design
│
├── src-tauri/               # Backend (Rust + Tauri)
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.rs          # App entry point
│       ├── lib.rs           # Core initialization & Tauri commands
│       ├── config.rs        # Configuration persistence
│       ├── presence.rs      # Activity simulation logic
│       ├── mouse.rs         # Human-like mouse movement
│       ├── input_monitor.rs # User idle detection (macOS)
│       └── tray.rs          # System tray menu
│
└── .claude/                 # Development tools
    ├── PROGRESS.log         # Task tracking
    └── CLAUDE.md            # Quick reference
```

## System Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────┐
│   SETTINGS WINDOW    │  Settings UI (HTML/CSS/JS)
│   (380x650px)        │  Always-on-top overlay
└──────────┬───────────┘
           │ Tauri IPC
           ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    TAURI COMMANDS                            │
│  get_config() | save_config() | toggle_enabled()            │
└──────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
           │
     ┌─────┴─────┐
     ▼           ▼
┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ CONFIG   │ │ PRESENCE KEEPER                          │
│          │ │                                          │
│ enabled  │ │ Loop: check work hours → check idle →   │
│ mode     │ │       decide activity → execute →       │
│ interval │ │       sleep interval                     │
│ variance │ └──────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
│ work hrs │            │
│ breaks   │      ┌─────┼─────┐
│ idle thr │      ▼     ▼     ▼
└──────────┘ ┌────────┐ ┌───┐ ┌──────────────┐
             │ MOUSE  │ │ KB│ │ INPUT MONITOR│
             │ Bézier │ │   │ │ CGEventTap   │
             │ Tremor │ │   │ │ Idle detect  │
             │Overshoot│ └───┘ └──────────────┘
             └────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SYSTEM TRAY                               │
│  Toggle | Pause 30m/1h | Resume | Settings | Quit           │
│  Active/Inactive icons | Pause countdown display            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Technologies

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | Tauri 2.x |
| Backend | Rust |
| Frontend | HTML/CSS/JavaScript |
| Build Tool | Vite 7.x |
| Input Control | enigo 0.3 |
| macOS Events | core-graphics, core-foundation |

## Configuration

Settings are stored at `~/.config/compliance/Compliance/config.json`:

```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "mode": "both",
  "interval_base_ms": 180000,
  "interval_variance": 0.5,
  "human_like": true,
  "idle_threshold_sec": 3,
  "work_hours": {
    "enabled": false,
    "start": "09:00",
    "end": "18:00"
  },
  "natural_breaks": {
    "enabled": false,
    "chance": 0.05,
    "duration_min": 15,
    "duration_max": 30
  }
}
```

## Development

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+
- Rust 1.70+
- Tauri CLI

### Commands

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Development mode
npm run tauri:dev

# Production build
npm run tauri:build

# Type check, lint, and format
npm run check

# Generate app icons
npm run tauri:icon
```

## Platform Support

| Platform | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| macOS | Full | CGEventTap for idle detection |
| Windows | Partial | Idle detection not implemented |
| Linux | Partial | Idle detection not implemented |

## Permissions

- **macOS**: Requires "Input Monitoring" accessibility permission for idle detection

## License

MIT

## README.md Review: Clarity and Quality Assessment

### Overall Assessment

The README is well-structured and professional. It effectively communicates what the application does and how it works. Below are specific observations and suggestions organized by your review criteria.

---

### 1. Clarity and Organization

**Strengths:**
- Clear opening tagline and one-sentence description
- Features are presented as scannable bullet points
- Tables are used appropriately for structured data

**Issues Found:**

1. **Redundant structure sections**: There are two architecture representations (file tree + system diagram). Consider consolidating under a single "Architecture" heading with subheadings like "Project Structure" and "System Overview."

2. **Configuration file path inconsistency**: The path `~/.config/compliance/Compliance/config.json` has an unusual double "compliance/Compliance" pattern. Verify this is accurate—it may confuse users.

---

### 2. Architecture Diagram Readability

**Strengths:**
- ASCII diagram is reasonably clear for a text-based format
- Shows data flow with directional arrows

**Issues Found:**

1. **Diagram width inconsistency**: Some boxes extend to different widths, creating visual asymmetry. The `TAURI COMMANDS` box is wider than `SETTINGS WINDOW`, which then connects to an even wider `PRESENCE KEEPER` block.

2. **Abbreviation "KB"**: The label `│ KB│` is unclear. Expand to "KEYBOARD" or at minimum add a label below similar to other boxes.

3. **Orphaned CONFIG box**: The CONFIG box appears connected to the flow but its relationship to PRESENCE KEEPER is not explicitly shown with an arrow. It seems to be read by PRESENCE KEEPER but this is implied, not drawn.

**Suggested improvement for KB section:**
```
┌────────────┐
│  KEYBOARD  │
│ Shift key  │
└────────────┘
```

---

### 3. Section Order

The current order is logical:
1. Title/Description → 2. Features → 3. Architecture → 4. Technologies → 5. Configuration → 6. Development → 7. Platform Support → 8. Permissions → 9. License

**Suggestion:** Consider moving "Platform Support" and "Permissions" before "Development" since end users (non-developers) need to know these before installing, whereas developers reading "Development" are already committed to contributing.

Recommended order:
1. Title/Description
2. Features
3. Platform Support
4. Permissions
5. Architecture
6. Technologies
7. Configuration
8. Development
9. License

---

### 4. Writing Conciseness

**Issues Found:**

1. **Line 9** - "Human-like mouse movements using Bézier curves with natural tremor and occasional overshoot" is technically accurate but dense. Consider:
   > "Human-like mouse movements with natural tremor and occasional overshoot"

   (Bézier curves are an implementation detail most users don't need.)

2. **Line 14** - "Quick access menu with pause/resume controls" could be:
   > "Quick-access menu with pause/resume"

3. **Lines 63-65** - The loop description in the diagram is hard to parse. Consider restructuring:
   ```
   │ Loop:                                  │
   │   1. Check work hours                  │
   │   2. Check idle state                  │
   │   3. Execute activity                  │
   │   4. Sleep interval                    │
   ```

---

### 5. Grammar and Formatting Issues

1. **Line 3** - The tagline uses an em dash incorrectly. Should be:
   > `> *Always Compliant* — Keep your status always available`
   
   (Use proper em dash `—` instead of hyphen `-`)

2. **Line 10** - Missing article:
   > "Shift key press/release (no visible output)"
   
   Better as:
   > "Shift-key press/release (no visible output)"

3. **Line 152-154** - Table formatting is correct but the "Partial" entries could benefit from consistency:
   > "Planned" or "Basic" instead of "Partial" to clarify intent

4. **Line 35-37** - The `.claude/` directory section may not belong in a public README since these are development tools specific to Claude Code workflows. Consider removing or moving to a CONTRIBUTING.md file.

---

### Summary of Recommended Changes

| Priority | Issue | Location |
|----------|-------|----------|
| High | Remove or relocate `.claude/` from public architecture tree | Lines 35-37 |
| Medium | Expand "KB" label in system diagram | Line 71 |
| Medium | Verify config path accuracy (`compliance/Compliance`) | Line 97 |
| Low | Use proper em dash in tagline | Line 3 |
| Low | Reorder sections (Platform/Permissions before Development) | Overall |
| Low | Simplify Bézier curves mention | Line 9 |

The README is professional and functional as-is. These suggestions would elevate it from good to excellent.

# Compliance

> *Always Compliant* - Keep your status always available

A lightweight desktop utility that simulates user activity to prevent "Away" status in collaboration tools like Microsoft Teams.

## Features

- **Mouse Jiggler**: Human-like mouse movements using Bézier curves with natural tremor and occasional overshoot
- **Keyboard Simulation**: Shift key press/release (no visible output)
- **Idle Detection**: Only activates when user is truly idle (configurable threshold)
- **Work Hours Scheduling**: Restrict activity to work hours
- **Natural Breaks**: Simulate coffee breaks for realistic presence patterns
- **System Tray Integration**: Quick access menu with pause/resume controls

## Architecture

```
compliance/
├── src/                      # Frontend (HTML/CSS/JavaScript)
│   ├── index.html           # Settings window UI
│   ├── main.js              # UI controller & IPC communication
│   └── styles.css           # Dark mode, macOS-like design
│
├── src-tauri/               # Backend (Rust + Tauri)
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.rs          # App entry point
│       ├── lib.rs           # Core initialization & Tauri commands
│       ├── config.rs        # Configuration persistence
│       ├── presence.rs      # Activity simulation logic
│       ├── mouse.rs         # Human-like mouse movement
│       ├── input_monitor.rs # User idle detection (macOS)
│       └── tray.rs          # System tray menu
│
└── .claude/                 # Development tools
    ├── PROGRESS.log         # Task tracking
    └── CLAUDE.md            # Quick reference
```

## System Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────┐
│   SETTINGS WINDOW    │  Settings UI (HTML/CSS/JS)
│   (380x650px)        │  Always-on-top overlay
└──────────┬───────────┘
           │ Tauri IPC
           ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    TAURI COMMANDS                            │
│  get_config() | save_config() | toggle_enabled()            │
└──────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
           │
     ┌─────┴─────┐
     ▼           ▼
┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ CONFIG   │ │ PRESENCE KEEPER                          │
│          │ │                                          │
│ enabled  │ │ Loop: check work hours → check idle →   │
│ mode     │ │       decide activity → execute →       │
│ interval │ │       sleep interval                     │
│ variance │ └──────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
│ work hrs │            │
│ breaks   │      ┌─────┼─────┐
│ idle thr │      ▼     ▼     ▼
└──────────┘ ┌────────┐ ┌───┐ ┌──────────────┐
             │ MOUSE  │ │ KB│ │ INPUT MONITOR│
             │ Bézier │ │   │ │ CGEventTap   │
             │ Tremor │ │   │ │ Idle detect  │
             │Overshoot│ └───┘ └──────────────┘
             └────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SYSTEM TRAY                               │
│  Toggle | Pause 30m/1h | Resume | Settings | Quit           │
│  Active/Inactive icons | Pause countdown display            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Technologies

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | Tauri 2.x |
| Backend | Rust |
| Frontend | HTML/CSS/JavaScript |
| Build Tool | Vite 7.x |
| Input Control | enigo 0.3 |
| macOS Events | core-graphics, core-foundation |

## Configuration

Settings are stored at `~/.config/compliance/Compliance/config.json`:

```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "mode": "both",
  "interval_base_ms": 180000,
  "interval_variance": 0.5,
  "human_like": true,
  "idle_threshold_sec": 3,
  "work_hours": {
    "enabled": false,
    "start": "09:00",
    "end": "18:00"
  },
  "natural_breaks": {
    "enabled": false,
    "chance": 0.05,
    "duration_min": 15,
    "duration_max": 30
  }
}
```

## Development

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+
- Rust 1.70+
- Tauri CLI

### Commands

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Development mode
npm run tauri:dev

# Production build
npm run tauri:build

# Type check, lint, and format
npm run check

# Generate app icons
npm run tauri:icon
```

## Platform Support

| Platform | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| macOS | Full | CGEventTap for idle detection |
| Windows | Partial | Idle detection not implemented |
| Linux | Partial | Idle detection not implemented |

## Permissions

- **macOS**: Requires "Input Monitoring" accessibility permission for idle detection

## License

MIT

# Compliance

> *Always Compliant* — Keep your status always available

A lightweight desktop utility that simulates user activity to prevent "Away" status in collaboration tools like Microsoft Teams.

## Features

- **Mouse Jiggler**: Human-like mouse movements using Bézier curves with natural tremor and occasional overshoot
- **Keyboard Simulation**: Shift key press/release (no visible output)
- **Idle Detection**: Only activates when user is truly idle (configurable threshold)
- **Work Hours Scheduling**: Restrict activity to work hours
- **Natural Breaks**: Simulate coffee breaks for realistic presence patterns
- **System Tray Integration**: Quick access menu with pause/resume controls

## Architecture

```
compliance/
├── src/                      # Frontend (HTML/CSS/JavaScript)
│   ├── index.html           # Settings window UI
│   ├── main.js              # UI controller & IPC communication
│   └── styles.css           # Dark mode, macOS-like design
│
├── src-tauri/               # Backend (Rust + Tauri)
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.rs          # App entry point
│       ├── lib.rs           # Core initialization & Tauri commands
│       ├── config.rs        # Configuration persistence
│       ├── presence.rs      # Activity simulation logic
│       ├── mouse.rs         # Human-like mouse movement
│       ├── input_monitor.rs # User idle detection (macOS)
│       └── tray.rs          # System tray menu
│
├── src-tauri/
│   ├── icons/               # App and tray icons
│   ├── Cargo.toml           # Rust dependencies
│   └── tauri.conf.json      # Tauri configuration
│
├── package.json             # Node dependencies & scripts
└── tsconfig.json            # TypeScript configuration
